backend.text: Configure DungBeetle's Redis connection with `ioredis`: `new Redis({ host, port, password, tls: {}, retryStrategy: times => Math.min(times * 50, 2000) })`.
backend.subSection: PART E — Full-Stack Integration & Project Synthesis (Q401–Q500)

backend.text: What is `pg` vs `postgres.js` vs `Knex` for PostgreSQL in Node.js? Why does DungBeetle use `postgres.js`?
backend.subSection: PART E — Full-Stack Integration & Project Synthesis (Q401–Q500)

backend.text: Implement DungBeetle's job queue worker with `postgres.js`: `SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED WHERE status='pending' ORDER BY priority DESC LIMIT 1`.
backend.subSection: PART E — Full-Stack Integration & Project Synthesis (Q401–Q500)

backend.text: What is `pino` logger in Node.js? How does DungBeetle configure it for structured JSON logging with trace_id?
backend.subSection: PART E — Full-Stack Integration & Project Synthesis (Q401–Q500)

backend.text: Configure pino for DungBeetle: `pino({ level: 'info', base: { service: 'dung-beetle', env: process.env.NODE_ENV } })`. Add `trace_id` via `AsyncLocalStorage`.
backend.subSection: PART E — Full-Stack Integration & Project Synthesis (Q401–Q500)

backend.text: What is `opentelemetry-js`? How does DungBeetle auto-instrument Express, Prisma, and Redis with zero code changes?
backend.subSection: PART E — Full-Stack Integration & Project Synthesis (Q401–Q500)

backend.text: Bootstrap OpenTelemetry in DungBeetle: `sdk.start()` before any imports, configure OTLP exporter to OpenTrace Collector, enable auto-instrumentation packages.
backend.subSection: PART E — Full-Stack Integration & Project Synthesis (Q401–Q500)

backend.text: What is `helmet` in Express/Fastify? What security headers does DungBeetle set automatically?
backend.subSection: PART E — Full-Stack Integration & Project Synthesis (Q401–Q500)

backend.text: Add `helmet` to DungBeetle's Express server: `app.use(helmet())`. Verify `X-Content-Type-Options`, `X-Frame-Options`, `Strict-Transport-Security` are set.
backend.subSection: PART E — Full-Stack Integration & Project Synthesis (Q401–Q500)

backend.text: What is `compression` middleware in Express? When does DungBeetle enable gzip compression for API responses?
backend.subSection: PART E — Full-Stack Integration & Project Synthesis (Q401–Q500)

backend.text: Add response compression to DungBeetle: `app.use(compression({ threshold: 1024 }))`. Only compress responses > 1KB.
backend.subSection: PART E — Full-Stack Integration & Project Synthesis (Q401–Q500)

backend.text: What is `express-rate-limit`? How does DungBeetle implement basic rate limiting without Redis?
backend.subSection: PART E — Full-Stack Integration & Project Synthesis (Q401–Q500)

backend.text: Add rate limiting to DungBeetle's API: `rateLimit({ windowMs: 60000, max: 100, standardHeaders: true })`. The basic version without Redis.
backend.subSection: PART E — Full-Stack Integration & Project Synthesis (Q401–Q500)

backend.text: What is `morgan` vs `pino-http` for HTTP access logging in Node.js? Why does DungBeetle use `pino-http`?
backend.subSection: PART E — Full-Stack Integration & Project Synthesis (Q401–Q500)

backend.text: Add pino-http to DungBeetle: `app.use(pinoHttp({ logger, serializers: { req: (req) => ({ method: req.method, url: req.url, traceId: req.headers['x-trace-id'] }) } }))`.
backend.subSection: PART E — Full-Stack Integration & Project Synthesis (Q401–Q500)

backend.text: What is `dotenv` vs environment variables vs Kubernetes Secrets for configuration? Why does DungBeetle use `dotenv` only in development?
backend.subSection: PART E — Full-Stack Integration & Project Synthesis (Q401–Q500)

backend.text: Configure DungBeetle's environment: `dotenv.config()` in development, Kubernetes Secrets via env vars in production. Use `zod.parse(process.env)` to validate required env vars.
backend.subSection: PART E — Full-Stack Integration & Project Synthesis (Q401–Q500)

backend.text: What is `pkg` vs `nexe` vs compiling to native binary for Node.js distribution? When would DungBeetle ship as a single binary?
backend.subSection: PART E — Full-Stack Integration & Project Synthesis (Q401–Q500)

backend.text: What is `turborepo` for monorepo management? How does the 5-project Infraspec portfolio use Turborepo for shared packages and parallel builds?
backend.subSection: PART E — Full-Stack Integration & Project Synthesis (Q401–Q500)

backend.text: Final synthesis: You are at the Infraspec technical interview. Walk through OpenTrace's technology stack in 5 minutes: Go (Collector, Processor, Query, Storage), TypeScript (API Gateway, UI, SDK), Next.js App Router (UI), React Query (data fetching), Tailwind + Shadcn (design system), WebSocket (live tail), PostgreSQL + ClickHouse + Redis (storage), Kafka (pipeline), Kubernetes (deployment). Justify every choice.
backend.subSection: PART E — Full-Stack Integration & Project Synthesis (Q401–Q500)

backend.text: What is Docker? What problem does it solve vs installing software directly on a host?
What is a Docker image? What is a Docker container? What is the relationship between them?
What is a Docker layer? How does layer caching work? Why does layer order matter in a Dockerfile?
What is the difference between `CMD` and `ENTRYPOINT` in a Dockerfile? When do you use each?
Write a Dockerfile for OpenTrace Collector: single-stage build, `FROM golang:1.23`, `COPY go.mod go.sum`, `RUN go mod download`, `COPY .`, `RUN go build`, `CMD`.
What is a multi-stage Docker build? Why does it reduce image size from 900MB to ~20MB?
Write a multi-stage Dockerfile for OpenTrace Collector: builder stage compiles Go binary, runner stage uses `gcr.io/distroless/static`. Compare image sizes.
What is `gcr.io/distroless/static` vs `alpine` vs `scratch` as a base image? What are the security and size tradeoffs?
Write a production Dockerfile for OpenTrace Collector: multi-stage, distroless base, non-root user `USER 65534:65534`, read-only filesystem, `HEALTHCHECK`.
What is a `.dockerignore` file? What should OpenTrace exclude: `.git`, `vendor`, `*.test`, `node_modules`, `dist`?
Write `.dockerignore` for OpenTrace: exclude git, test files, local config, coverage reports. Show impact on build context size.
What is `docker build --cache-from`? How does OpenTrace's CI use it to speed up builds by reusing layers from the previous image?
Use BuildKit cache mounts in OpenTrace's Dockerfile: `RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/go/pkg/mod go build`. This caches module downloads across builds.
What is BuildKit? What does it enable over the classic Docker builder: parallel stages, secret mounts, cache mounts, SSH forwarding?
Enable BuildKit for OpenTrace's Docker build: `DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 docker build .` or `docker buildx build .`.
What is a Docker volume? What is a bind mount? What is `tmpfs`? When does OpenTrace use each?
Run OpenTrace Collector with a bind mount for config: `docker run -v /etc/openTrace:/config:ro openTrace-collector`.
What is `docker compose`? What is a `docker-compose.yml`? When does OpenTrace use it for local development?
Write a `docker-compose.yml` for OpenTrace local dev: Collector, Kafka (Redpanda), ClickHouse, PostgreSQL, Redis. Set `depends_on` with health checks.
What is `HEALTHCHECK` in a Dockerfile? How does OpenTrace's Collector define its health check?
Add `HEALTHCHECK` to OpenTrace Collector's Dockerfile: `HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --retries=3 CMD wget -qO- http://localhost:8080/health/live || exit 1`.
What is `docker inspect`? How do you find OpenTrace Collector's container IP, port bindings, and environment variables?
Use `docker inspect openTrace-collector --format '{{.NetworkSettings.IPAddress}}'` to get the container IP.
What is `docker stats`? How do you monitor OpenTrace Collector's CPU and memory usage in real-time?
Run `docker stats openTrace-collector --format "CPU: {{.CPUPerc}}, MEM: {{.MemUsage}}"` to monitor resource usage.
What is `docker exec`? How do you get a shell into a running OpenTrace container for debugging?
Debug OpenTrace container: `docker exec -it openTrace-collector /bin/sh`. But distroless has no shell — how do you debug a distroless container?
How do you debug a distroless container? Options: `docker debug`, `kubectl debug`, or ephemeral container with `busybox`.
Debug distroless OpenTrace container: `docker run --rm --pid container:openTrace-collector --network container:openTrace-collector -it busybox sh`.
What is a Docker network? What are the modes: bridge, host, overlay, none? When does OpenTrace use host networking for maximum performance?
Run OpenTrace Collector with host networking: `docker run --network host openTrace-collector`. When is this appropriate and when is it a security risk?
What is `docker system prune`? What does it clean? How often should OpenTrace's CI runners run it?
What is a Docker registry? What is ECR (AWS), GHCR (GitHub), Docker Hub? How does OpenTrace push to ECR?
Push OpenTrace image to ECR: `aws ecr get-login-password | docker login --username AWS --password-stdin $ECR_URL && docker push $ECR_URL/openTrace-collector:$TAG`.
What is image vulnerability scanning? What is `trivy`? What is `grype`? How does OpenTrace's CI block deploys with CRITICAL CVEs?
Run `trivy image openTrace-collector:latest --severity CRITICAL,HIGH --exit-code 1` in CI. This fails the build if any critical vulnerabilities are found.
What is `docker sbom` (Software Bill of Materials)? When does OpenTrace generate an SBOM for security compliance?
What is OCI (Open Container Initiative) image format? How does it differ from Docker's image format?
Use `docker buildx` for multi-platform builds: `docker buildx build --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64 -t openTrace-collector:latest --push .`.
Distroless vs Alpine vs scratch for OpenTrace's base image: compare size, security surface, debugging ability, and compatibility.
What is running as non-root in Docker? Why does OpenTrace use `USER 65534:65534` (nobody)?
Add non-root user to OpenTrace Dockerfile: `USER 65534:65534` in the runner stage. Verify: `docker run openTrace-collector whoami` → `nobody`.
What is a read-only root filesystem in Docker? Why does OpenTrace use `--read-only` for the Collector container?
Run OpenTrace with read-only filesystem: `docker run --read-only --tmpfs /tmp openTrace-collector`. What directories need tmpfs mounts?
What is `--cap-drop ALL --cap-add NET_BIND_SERVICE` for Docker? How does OpenTrace minimize Linux capabilities?
Run OpenTrace Collector with dropped capabilities: `docker run --cap-drop ALL --security-opt no-new-privileges openTrace-collector`.
What is a seccomp profile in Docker? What is the default Docker seccomp profile? How does OpenTrace use a custom restricted profile?
What are Docker secrets vs environment variables? Why should OpenTrace's database password not be in `ENV` in the Dockerfile?
Pass OpenTrace's PostgreSQL password via Docker secret: `docker run --secret id=pg_password,src=./pg_password.txt openTrace-collector`. Read from `/run/secrets/pg_password`.
What is `--no-cache` in Docker build? When does OpenTrace's CI force a clean build to pick up security patches?
What is image pinning? Why does OpenTrace pin base images to digest (`FROM golang:1.23@sha256:abc...`) instead of tags?
Pin OpenTrace's base image: `FROM golang:1.23.4@sha256:abc123... AS builder`. This prevents unexpected base image changes.
What is Hadolint? How does OpenTrace use it to lint Dockerfiles in CI?
Add Hadolint to OpenTrace CI: `hadolint Dockerfile --ignore DL3008 --ignore DL3009`. Show common Dockerfile anti-patterns it catches.
What is `COPY --chown` vs `RUN chown` in Dockerfile? Why does `COPY --chown` produce fewer layers?
Use `COPY --chown=65534:65534` in OpenTrace Dockerfile to set file ownership in one step instead of a separate `RUN chown`.
What is Docker Content Trust (DCT)? How does it verify image signatures?
What is `docker login` with OIDC? How does OpenTrace's CI authenticate to ECR using GitHub Actions OIDC without stored credentials?
Configure ECR authentication in OpenTrace's GitHub Actions: use `aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials` with OIDC role, no static AWS keys.
What is layer deduplication in Docker? How does OpenTrace's monorepo share base layers between Collector, Processor, and Query Service images?
Create a shared base image for OpenTrace: `openTrace-base` with Go toolchain + common dependencies. All services `FROM openTrace-base AS builder`.
What is `docker image ls --digests`? How do you verify OpenTrace's deployed image matches what CI built?
Verify OpenTrace deployment integrity: compare `docker image ls openTrace-collector --digests` on the deployed node with the CI-produced digest.
What is `dive` for Docker image analysis? How do you find which Dockerfile instruction adds the most size?
Run `dive openTrace-collector:latest` to analyze layer efficiency. Identify wasted space (e.g., test files not cleaned up, apt cache not removed).
What is `docker buildx bake`? How does OpenTrace build all 7 component images in parallel?
Write a `docker-bake.hcl` for OpenTrace: define 7 targets (collector, processor, query, gateway, ui, sdk, init), build all in parallel.
What is an OCI artifact? How does OpenTrace store Helm charts and SBOM alongside container images in ECR?
What is `docker manifest`? How does OpenTrace create a multi-arch manifest list pointing to amd64 and arm64 images?
Create OpenTrace multi-arch manifest: `docker manifest create openTrace:latest openTrace:amd64 openTrace:arm64`. Push with `docker manifest push`.
What is `--platform` in Docker `FROM` for cross-compilation? How does OpenTrace build ARM64 images on AMD64 CI runners?
Add QEMU emulation for cross-platform builds: `docker run --rm --privileged multiarch/qemu-user-static --reset -p yes`. Then `docker buildx build --platform linux/arm64`.
What is `EXPOSE` in Dockerfile? Is it documentation or does it actually open a port?
Add `EXPOSE 4317/tcp` (gRPC) and `EXPOSE 8080/tcp` (HTTP) to OpenTrace Collector Dockerfile. Explain it's metadata only.
What is `ARG` vs `ENV` in Dockerfile? When does OpenTrace use `ARG` for build-time variables like git SHA?
Pass git SHA to OpenTrace image: `ARG GIT_SHA`, `ENV APP_VERSION=$GIT_SHA`, `docker build --build-arg GIT_SHA=$(git rev-parse HEAD)`.
What is `docker network create`? How does OpenTrace's docker-compose create an isolated network for all services?
What is `--init` in Docker run? Why does OpenTrace use it to handle zombie processes in the container?
OpenTrace Collector container exits with code 137 (OOMKilled). How do you diagnose: check `docker inspect --format '{{.State.OOMKilled}}'`, then tune memory limits.
Design OpenTrace's complete Docker strategy: multi-stage builds, distroless base, non-root, read-only FS, BuildKit cache mounts, multi-arch, trivy scanning, ECR OIDC push, size target < 25MB.
What is Kubernetes? What problem does it solve vs running Docker containers directly?
What is a Pod? What is a Deployment? What is a Service? What is an Ingress? How do they relate?
What is the Kubernetes control plane? What are: kube-apiserver, etcd, kube-scheduler, kube-controller-manager?
What is a node? What is kubelet? What is kube-proxy? What is a container runtime (containerd)?
Write a minimal Kubernetes Deployment for OpenTrace Collector: 3 replicas, container image, ports, resource requests/limits.
Write a Kubernetes Service for OpenTrace Collector: `ClusterIP` type, port 4317 (gRPC), port 8080 (HTTP), selector by `app: openTrace-collector`.
Write a Kubernetes Ingress for OpenTrace: `collector.openTrace.dev → collector:4317`, `ui.openTrace.dev → ui:3000`. TLS with cert-manager annotation.
What is a ConfigMap? What is a Secret? How does OpenTrace use each?
Create a ConfigMap for OpenTrace's Kafka topic config. Create a Secret for the ClickHouse password. Mount both in the Collector pod.
What is a readiness probe vs liveness probe vs startup probe? What does each test?
Configure probes for OpenTrace Collector: readiness = `GET /health/ready` (Kafka connected), liveness = `GET /health/live` (process alive), startup = `GET /health/live` with `failureThreshold: 30`.
What are resource requests vs limits? What happens when a container exceeds its memory limit (OOMKill) vs CPU limit (throttled)?
Set resources for OpenTrace Collector: `requests: {cpu: 100m, memory: 256Mi}`, `limits: {cpu: 500m, memory: 1Gi}`. Set `GOMEMLIMIT=900MiB` env var.
What is HPA (Horizontal Pod Autoscaler)? What metrics can it scale on: CPU, memory, custom metrics?
Create HPA for OpenTrace Collector: scale 2–20 replicas based on custom metric `kafka_consumer_lag > 10000` from Prometheus Adapter.
What is VPA (Vertical Pod Autoscaler)? When would OpenTrace use VPA instead of or alongside HPA?
What is PodDisruptionBudget (PDB)? How does OpenTrace ensure at least 2 Collector replicas are always running during node maintenance?
Create PDB for OpenTrace Collector: `minAvailable: 2`. This prevents all pods from being evicted simultaneously.
What is rolling update strategy? What are `maxSurge` and `maxUnavailable`?
Configure rolling update for OpenTrace Collector: `maxSurge: 1`, `maxUnavailable: 0`. Zero-downtime deployment.
What is a StatefulSet? When does OpenTrace's ClickHouse cluster use StatefulSet instead of Deployment?
Write a StatefulSet for OpenTrace's ClickHouse: stable pod names (clickhouse-0, clickhouse-1), PersistentVolumeClaim templates.
What is a DaemonSet? When does OpenTrace use it for the log collector (Fluentd on every node)?
Write a DaemonSet for OpenTrace's Fluentd log collector: runs on every node, mounts `/var/log/containers` as hostPath.
What is a CronJob? How does OpenTrace use it for daily ClickHouse partition creation?
Write a CronJob for OpenTrace: runs at `0 0 * * *`, creates next month's ClickHouse partition, uses the same image as the Query Service.
What is a Kubernetes Job? When does OpenTrace use it for one-time database migrations?
Write a Kubernetes Job for OpenTrace's database migration: runs `golang-migrate` on startup, `restartPolicy: OnFailure`, `backoffLimit: 3`.
What is a ServiceAccount? What is IRSA (IAM Role for Service Account)? How does OpenTrace Collector write to S3 without hardcoded credentials?
Configure IRSA for OpenTrace Collector: create ServiceAccount with `eks.amazonaws.com/role-arn` annotation, IAM role with S3:PutObject, bind in pod spec.
What is a NetworkPolicy? How does OpenTrace restrict Collector to only communicate with Kafka and PostgreSQL?
Write NetworkPolicy for OpenTrace Collector: deny all ingress/egress, allow ingress on 4317, egress to Kafka (9092), PostgreSQL (5432), CoreDNS (53).
What is a PersistentVolume (PV) and PersistentVolumeClaim (PVC)? What is a StorageClass?
Write PVC for OpenTrace ClickHouse: `storageClassName: gp3`, `resources.requests.storage: 1Ti`, `accessModes: ReadWriteOnce`.
What is pod affinity vs anti-affinity? How does OpenTrace prevent two Collector pods from running on the same node?
Add pod anti-affinity to OpenTrace Collector: `podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution`, `topologyKey: kubernetes.io/hostname`.
What is `topologySpreadConstraints`? How does OpenTrace spread pods across AZs?
Add topologySpreadConstraints to OpenTrace: spread across 3 AZs with `maxSkew: 1`, `topologyKey: topology.kubernetes.io/zone`.
What is a Taint and Toleration? How does OpenTrace dedicate nodes to ClickHouse?
Add taint to ClickHouse nodes: `kubectl taint nodes ch-node dedicated=clickhouse:NoSchedule`. Add toleration to ClickHouse pods.
What is node affinity? How does OpenTrace schedule ClickHouse only on `r6i.4xlarge` (memory-optimized) nodes?
Add node affinity to OpenTrace ClickHouse: require `node.kubernetes.io/instance-type: r6i.4xlarge`.
What is `kubectl rollout`? What are `status`, `history`, `undo`? How does OpenTrace roll back a bad deployment?
Roll back OpenTrace Collector: `kubectl rollout undo deployment/openTrace-collector`. Verify: `kubectl rollout status deployment/openTrace-collector`.
What is `kubectl port-forward`? How does OpenTrace developer access pprof endpoint from a running pod?
Access OpenTrace Collector pprof: `kubectl port-forward pod/openTrace-collector-xyz 6060:6060`. Then `go tool pprof http://localhost:6060/debug/pprof/profile`.
What is `kubectl top pod`? What is `kubectl top node`? How does OpenTrace identify the highest memory consumer?
Find OpenTrace's memory-hungry pod: `kubectl top pod -n openTrace --sort-by=memory | head -5`.
What is QoS class in Kubernetes: Guaranteed, Burstable, BestEffort? What class does OpenTrace Collector get?
Debug OpenTrace pod eviction: `kubectl describe pod openTrace-collector-xyz | grep -A5 Conditions`. Check `OOMKilled` in `kubectl get events`.
What is cert-manager? How does it automate TLS certificate issuance for OpenTrace?
Write cert-manager ClusterIssuer for OpenTrace: Let's Encrypt production, DNS-01 challenge via Cloudflare. Annotate Ingress to auto-issue certificate.
What is ExternalDNS? How does it create Cloudflare DNS records when OpenTrace Ingress is deployed?
Deploy ExternalDNS for OpenTrace: configure with Cloudflare API token, `--domain-filter=openTrace.dev`, watch Ingress resources.
What is Kubernetes RBAC? What is a Role vs ClusterRole vs RoleBinding vs ClusterRoleBinding?
Create RBAC for OpenTrace Operator: ClusterRole with get/list/watch on deployments and configmaps, bind to ServiceAccount.
What is a Kubernetes Operator? What is a Custom Resource Definition (CRD)?
Define a CRD for OpenTrace: `OpenTraceCluster` resource with spec for replica counts, storage sizes, and Kafka bootstrap servers.
What is Helm? What is a chart? What is `values.yaml`? How does OpenTrace publish a Helm chart?
Create a Helm chart for OpenTrace Collector: templates for Deployment, Service, ServiceAccount, ConfigMap. Parametrize image tag and replica count in `values.yaml`.
What is Kustomize? How does OpenTrace use it for environment overlays (dev vs staging vs prod)?
Write Kustomize overlays for OpenTrace: base (common), dev (1 replica, small resources), prod (3 replicas, larger resources, HPA).
What is GitOps? What is ArgoCD? How does OpenTrace deploy from Git without direct `kubectl apply`?
Write ArgoCD Application for OpenTrace: sync from `github.com/org/openTrace-k8s`, auto-sync with prune, self-heal.
What is Flux? How does it compare to ArgoCD for OpenTrace's GitOps?
What is Argo Rollouts? What is a canary deployment? How does OpenTrace shift 5% → 20% → 100% of traffic to a new version?
Write Argo Rollouts canary for OpenTrace Collector: steps 5%, 20%, 100%, 5-minute pause at each step, auto-rollback if error rate > 1%.
What is Karpenter? How does it provision EC2 nodes faster than cluster-autoscaler for OpenTrace's traffic spikes?
What is Velero? How does it backup OpenTrace's Kubernetes resources and PVCs to S3?
Schedule Velero backup for OpenTrace: `velero schedule create openTrace-daily --schedule="0 2 * * *" --include-namespaces openTrace`.
What is the Kubernetes admission controller? What is OPA (Open Policy Agent) Gatekeeper? How does it enforce OpenTrace's security policies?
Write OPA Gatekeeper constraint for OpenTrace: require all pods to have resource limits set, require `runAsNonRoot: true`.
What is Kubernetes `ResourceQuota`? How does it prevent OpenTrace from consuming all cluster resources?
Write ResourceQuota for OpenTrace namespace: `requests.cpu: 16`, `requests.memory: 32Gi`, `limits.memory: 64Gi`, `count/pods: 100`.
What is `LimitRange`? How does it set default resource requests/limits for pods in OpenTrace's namespace?
Write LimitRange for OpenTrace: default container memory request 128Mi, default limit 512Mi. Prevents unbounded resource consumption.
What is a PriorityClass? How does OpenTrace's Collector get higher scheduling priority than the UI?
Create PriorityClass for OpenTrace: `collector-critical` (1000), `query-high` (500), `ui-normal` (0). Assign to respective deployments.
What is an init container? How does OpenTrace wait for Kafka to be ready before starting the Collector?
Add init container to OpenTrace Collector pod: busybox that runs `nc -z kafka:9092` in a loop until Kafka responds, then exits 0.
What is a sidecar container? How does OpenTrace use an OTel Collector sidecar vs DaemonSet approach?
What is ephemeral container in Kubernetes? How do you add a debug container to a running distroless pod?
Add ephemeral container to running OpenTrace pod: `kubectl debug -it openTrace-collector-xyz --image=busybox --target=openTrace-collector`.
What is `kubectl events`? How does OpenTrace's on-call use it to diagnose why a pod is not starting?
Diagnose failing OpenTrace pod: `kubectl get events -n openTrace --field-selector involvedObject.name=openTrace-collector-xyz --sort-by=.lastTimestamp`.
What is `kubectl drain` vs `kubectl cordon`? How do you safely remove a node from OpenTrace's cluster?
OpenTrace Collector pods are in `Pending` state. `kubectl describe pod` shows `Insufficient memory`. What are the options: add nodes, reduce requests, or use spot instances?
OpenTrace pod is in `CrashLoopBackOff`. How do you get the previous container logs: `kubectl logs --previous openTrace-collector-xyz`?
OpenTrace pod stuck in `Terminating` for 10 minutes. How do you force delete: `kubectl delete pod openTrace-collector-xyz --grace-period=0 --force`?
What is `kubectl diff`? How does OpenTrace verify what will change before applying a new Kubernetes manifest?
Dry-run OpenTrace deployment: `kubectl apply --dry-run=server -f deployment.yaml`. Then `kubectl diff -f deployment.yaml` to see changes.
What is the Kubernetes API rate limit? How does OpenTrace's CI avoid hitting the rate limit when deploying many resources at once?
What is `kubectl wait`? How does OpenTrace's deployment script wait for rollout to complete?
Wait for OpenTrace deployment: `kubectl wait deployment/openTrace-collector --for=condition=Available --timeout=5m -n openTrace`.
What is Kubernetes server-side apply? How does it handle conflict when multiple tools modify the same resource?
Use server-side apply for OpenTrace: `kubectl apply --server-side -f deployment.yaml`. Shows field manager ownership.
What is `kubectl get --watch`? How does OpenTrace's on-call monitor rolling deployment status in real-time?
Monitor OpenTrace deployment: `kubectl get pods -n openTrace -l app=openTrace-collector --watch` to see pod creation and readiness in real-time.
What is Kubernetes `imagePullPolicy: Always` vs `IfNotPresent`? When does OpenTrace use each?
What is the Kubernetes container resource model for GPU? How would OpenTrace's fraud detection ML inference use NVIDIA GPUs in Kubernetes?
What is a Kubernetes Operator lifecycle: watch → reconcile → update? How does OpenTrace's Operator handle a ClickHouse node failure?
Sketch OpenTrace Operator reconcile loop: if `openTraceCluster.spec.replicas != deployment.spec.replicas`, update deployment. If ClickHouse is not ready, requeue.
What is `kubectl scale`? How do you manually scale OpenTrace Collector during an incident?
Scale OpenTrace Collector during incident: `kubectl scale deployment/openTrace-collector --replicas=10 -n openTrace`. Monitor with `kubectl get pods -w`.
What is KEDA (Kubernetes Event-Driven Autoscaler)? How does it scale OpenTrace based on Kafka consumer lag without Prometheus Adapter?
Create KEDA ScaledObject for OpenTrace Collector: scale 2–20 replicas based on Kafka topic `spans` consumer group lag > 10000.
What is Kubernetes Pod Topology Spread? What is `whenUnsatisfiable: DoNotSchedule` vs `ScheduleAnyway`?
What is the Kubernetes scheduler extender? How would a custom scheduler prioritize OpenTrace's Collector pods on NUMA-aware nodes?
What is `kubectl config`? What is a kubeconfig context? How does OpenTrace's team manage access to dev/staging/prod clusters?
Use `kubectl config use-context openTrace-prod` to switch context. Verify: `kubectl config current-context`.
What is Service Mesh (Istio/Linkerd)? What does it add to OpenTrace's Kubernetes networking: mTLS, retries, circuit breaking, distributed tracing?
Enable Istio sidecar injection for OpenTrace namespace: `kubectl label namespace openTrace istio-injection=enabled`. This adds Envoy sidecar to every pod.
What is Kubernetes Secrets encryption at rest? How does OpenTrace enable envelope encryption for Secrets using AWS KMS?
What is External Secrets Operator? How does OpenTrace sync secrets from AWS Secrets Manager to Kubernetes Secrets automatically?
Write ExternalSecret for OpenTrace: sync `openTrace/clickhouse-password` from AWS Secrets Manager to Kubernetes Secret.
What is Kubernetes `ownerReferences`? How does garbage collection work when a Deployment is deleted?
What is `kubectl api-resources`? How do you list all CRDs installed in the OpenTrace cluster?
What is `kubectl explain`? How does OpenTrace developer explore the Kubernetes API schema?
What is Kubernetes audit logging? How does OpenTrace enable it to track who made what changes to the cluster?
Design the complete Kubernetes architecture for OpenTrace: 7 components, RBAC, NetworkPolicies, HPA, PDB, cert-manager, ExternalDNS, ArgoCD, Velero backup, node groups for different workloads.
What is Terraform? What is HCL? What is the `plan → apply` workflow?
What is Terraform state? Where does OpenTrace store it: S3 backend with DynamoDB lock?
Write Terraform backend config for OpenTrace: S3 state bucket, DynamoDB lock table, state encryption with KMS.
What is a Terraform module? How does OpenTrace use modules for reusable infrastructure components?
Write a Terraform module for OpenTrace ECS Fargate service: input variables for image, cpu, memory, env vars; output the service ARN.
What is `terraform plan` vs `terraform apply`? What is `terraform destroy`? What is `-target`?
Run Terraform plan for OpenTrace and save the plan: `terraform plan -out=openTrace.tfplan`. Apply the saved plan: `terraform apply openTrace.tfplan`.
What is `terraform import`? When does OpenTrace use it to bring manually created AWS resources under Terraform management?
What is `terraform workspace`? How does OpenTrace use workspaces for dev/staging/prod environments?
Use Terraform workspaces for OpenTrace: `terraform workspace new staging`, `terraform workspace select staging`, apply with workspace-specific `tfvars`.
What is `terraform fmt` and `terraform validate`? How does OpenTrace's CI enforce formatting?
Add Terraform quality checks to OpenTrace's CI: `terraform fmt -check`, `terraform validate`, `tflint --recursive`. Fail on any issue.
What is `tfsec` / `checkov`? How does OpenTrace scan Terraform for security misconfigurations?
Run `tfsec .` on OpenTrace's Terraform. Common findings: S3 bucket not encrypted, security groups too permissive, no MFA delete on S3.
What is `terraform output`? How does OpenTrace CI extract the ECS cluster ARN after apply?
Output OpenTrace's ECS cluster ARN: `output "cluster_arn" { value = aws_ecs_cluster.openTrace.arn }`. Use in `terraform output -raw cluster_arn`.
What is `depends_on` in Terraform? When does OpenTrace explicitly declare dependencies between resources?
Use `depends_on` in OpenTrace: ECS service depends on RDS being created first. `depends_on = [aws_db_instance.openTrace]`.
What is `lifecycle` in Terraform? What is `create_before_destroy`? How does OpenTrace use it for zero-downtime ECS service updates?
Add `lifecycle { create_before_destroy = true }` to OpenTrace's ECS service. This creates the new service before destroying the old.
What is `data source` in Terraform? How does OpenTrace reference existing AWS resources (existing VPC, existing EKS cluster)?
Use data source in OpenTrace Terraform: `data "aws_eks_cluster" "existing" { name = "my-cluster" }`. Reference: `data.aws_eks_cluster.existing.endpoint`.
What is `for_each` vs `count` in Terraform? How does OpenTrace create 3 subnets in 3 AZs using `for_each`?
Create OpenTrace subnets with `for_each`:
What is `dynamic` block in Terraform? When does OpenTrace use it for variable-length ingress rules?
Use dynamic block for OpenTrace security group ingress rules:
What is `local` value in Terraform? How does OpenTrace use locals to avoid repeating expressions?
Define locals for OpenTrace: `locals { name_prefix = "${var.environment}-openTrace", common_tags = { Environment = var.environment, Project = "openTrace" } }`.
What is `variable` vs `locals` vs `output` in Terraform? When does OpenTrace expose outputs to other modules?
What is Terragrunt? How does OpenTrace use it to reduce Terraform boilerplate and manage multiple environments?
Write Terragrunt config for OpenTrace: root `terragrunt.hcl` with S3 backend config, environment `terragrunt.hcl` with variable overrides.
What is Terraform Cloud / Terraform Enterprise? When does OpenTrace use it for team collaboration and audit trail?
What is `terraform state mv`? When does OpenTrace use it to rename a resource without destroying and recreating it?
Move OpenTrace's ECS cluster in Terraform state: `terraform state mv aws_ecs_cluster.openTrace aws_ecs_cluster.openTrace_v2`.
What is `terraform refresh`? What is `terraform state pull`? When does OpenTrace use them for drift detection?
What is configuration drift? How does OpenTrace detect when AWS resources differ from Terraform state?
Detect drift in OpenTrace: `terraform plan -refresh-only` shows what needs to be refreshed. Alert if drift detected in CI.
What is `terraform test`? What is a provider mock? How does OpenTrace unit-test Terraform modules?
Write a Terraform test for OpenTrace's ECS module: assert that the service has the correct CPU and memory, uses the correct VPC.
What is CDK for Terraform (CDKTF)? When would OpenTrace use TypeScript to write Terraform instead of HCL?
What is OpenTofu (open-source Terraform fork)? What changed with HashiCorp's BSL license?
What is Pulumi? How does it compare to Terraform for OpenTrace? Why does the plan choose Terraform?
Terraform vs AWS CDK vs Pulumi vs CloudFormation for OpenTrace's infrastructure: compare developer experience, vendor lock-in, and state management.
What is `terraform plan -destroy`? When does OpenTrace use it to safely preview destroying staging infrastructure?
Preview destroying OpenTrace staging: `terraform plan -destroy -var-file=staging.tfvars`. Review output before confirming.
What is `terraform providers lock`? How does OpenTrace pin provider versions for reproducible infrastructure?
Lock provider versions for OpenTrace: `terraform providers lock -platform=linux_amd64 -platform=darwin_amd64`. Commit `.terraform.lock.hcl`.
What is `terraform apply -auto-approve`? When does OpenTrace's CI use it safely vs requiring manual approval?
What is Infracost? How does OpenTrace estimate AWS cost changes before applying Terraform changes?
Add Infracost to OpenTrace's CI: `infracost breakdown --path . --format json`. Show cost diff in PR comment. Alert if monthly cost increases > $500.
What is AWS ECS Fargate? What problem does it solve vs EC2? When does OpenTrace use Fargate for stateless services?
Write Terraform for OpenTrace ECS Fargate task definition: container definition with image, CPU (512), memory (1024), port mappings, environment variables from Secrets Manager.
What is AWS RDS? What is Multi-AZ RDS? What is the difference between RDS PostgreSQL and Aurora PostgreSQL?
Write Terraform for OpenTrace PostgreSQL RDS: `db.t3.medium`, Multi-AZ, encrypted, in private subnet group, security group allowing only app security group.
What is AWS ElastiCache? What is Redis on ElastiCache vs self-managed Redis? When does OpenTrace use ElastiCache?
Write Terraform for OpenTrace Redis ElastiCache: `cache.r7g.large`, cluster mode disabled, encryption in transit + at rest, replication group with 1 replica.
What is AWS MSK (Managed Streaming for Kafka)? How does it compare to self-managed Kafka for OpenTrace?
Write Terraform for OpenTrace MSK cluster: 3 brokers, `kafka.m5.large`, storage 500GB, encryption, SASL/SCRAM authentication.
What is AWS S3? What are buckets, objects, keys, versioning, lifecycle policies?
Write Terraform for OpenTrace S3 bucket: versioning enabled, server-side encryption (AES-256), lifecycle policy (delete versions > 30 days), block all public access.
What is S3 presigned URL? How does OpenTrace generate them for secure trace export downloads?
Generate S3 presigned URL in Go for OpenTrace: `s3.PresignGetObject(ctx, bucket, key, 1*time.Hour)`. Include in export response.
What is S3 multipart upload? When does OpenTrace use it for exporting > 5MB trace archives?
Implement S3 multipart upload for OpenTrace's trace export: split into 10MB parts, upload in parallel (5 concurrent), complete multipart upload.
What is S3 Intelligent-Tiering? When does OpenTrace use it for automatically tiering span archives from S3 Standard to Glacier?
What is AWS VPC? What are public vs private subnets? What is a NAT Gateway?
Write Terraform for OpenTrace VPC: 3 AZs, public subnets (load balancers), private subnets (services), database subnets (RDS/Redis), NAT Gateway per AZ.
What is a security group vs NACL in AWS? How does OpenTrace use security groups for micro-segmentation?
Write Terraform security groups for OpenTrace: ALB SG (0.0.0.0/0 on 443), Collector SG (only from ALB SG), RDS SG (only from Collector SG).
What is AWS CloudFront? How does OpenTrace use it to cache the UI static assets globally?
Write Terraform for OpenTrace CloudFront: origin = S3 bucket, cache policy for static assets (max-age=31536000), default root object `index.html`.
What is AWS ACM (Certificate Manager)? How does OpenTrace provision TLS certificates for CloudFront and ALB?
Write Terraform for OpenTrace ACM certificate: `*.openTrace.dev`, DNS validation via Route53, wait for validation.
What is AWS Route53? What is a hosted zone? How does OpenTrace manage DNS in Terraform?
Write Terraform for OpenTrace Route53: A record alias for ALB (`collector.openTrace.dev → ALB DNS`), CNAME for CloudFront (`ui.openTrace.dev → CloudFront`).
What is AWS EKS? What is a node group? What is Fargate profile on EKS?
Write Terraform for OpenTrace EKS cluster: version 1.29, managed node groups (general: m5.xlarge, memory: r6i.4xlarge), enable IRSA, cluster autoscaler.
What is AWS IAM? What is OIDC federation? How does EKS IRSA work?
Write Terraform for OpenTrace IRSA: create OIDC provider for EKS, IAM role with assume-role policy for service account, S3 write policy.
What is AWS Secrets Manager vs SSM Parameter Store? When does OpenTrace use each?
Write Terraform for OpenTrace Secrets Manager: store ClickHouse password, enable rotation every 30 days, grant read access to ECS task role.
What is AWS CloudWatch? What is CloudWatch Logs? When does OpenTrace use CloudWatch vs Loki?
Write Terraform for OpenTrace CloudWatch log group: 30-day retention, KMS encryption, subscription filter to forward to Loki.
What is AWS SQS? When would OpenTrace use SQS vs Kafka for simple async work queues?
Write Terraform for OpenTrace SQS dead letter queue: main queue with `visibilityTimeout=30`, DLQ with `maxReceiveCount=3`.
What is AWS SNS? How does OpenTrace use it for fan-out notifications to multiple SQS queues?
What is AWS Lambda? When does OpenTrace use Lambda for event-driven processing?
Write Terraform for OpenTrace Lambda: S3 trigger on new trace export, Lambda reads and indexes in Elasticsearch. Function URL for HTTP access.
What is AWS ALB? What is target group? What is listener rule? How does OpenTrace route traffic?
Write Terraform for OpenTrace ALB: HTTPS listener (port 443), forward rules by host header (collector.openTrace.dev → Collector target group).
What is AWS CloudFront distribution? What is origin access identity (OAI) / origin access control (OAC)?
What is AWS Cost Explorer? How does OpenTrace identify the top 3 cost drivers monthly?
What is Cloudflare R2 vs AWS S3? What is the zero-egress advantage for OpenTrace's trace downloads?
Write Terraform for Cloudflare R2 bucket for OpenTrace: `cloudflare_r2_bucket` resource, CORS policy for UI downloads.
What is AWS Auto Scaling group? When does OpenTrace use ASG with spot instances for cost-optimized batch processing?
What is Reserved Instances vs Spot Instances vs On-Demand? What is OpenTrace's cost optimization strategy for each component?
What is AWS Graviton (ARM64)? What is the cost savings vs x86? Why does OpenTrace build ARM64 images?
AWS ECS Fargate vs EKS for OpenTrace: compare operational complexity, cost, scaling speed, and control.
What is AWS Bedrock? When would OpenTrace use it for the AI-native stack (span anomaly detection)?

Design OpenTrace's complete AWS architecture: VPC (3 AZs), EKS (node groups), RDS (Multi-AZ), ElastiCache (replicated), MSK (3 brokers), S3 (span archives), CloudFront (UI), Route53 (DNS), ACM (TLS). Draw the network flow.
What is CI/CD? What is continuous integration vs continuous delivery vs continuous deployment?
What is GitHub Actions? What is a workflow? What is a job? What is a step?
Write a basic GitHub Actions workflow for OpenTrace: trigger on `push` to `main`, jobs: lint, test, build Docker image.
What is `on: [push, pull_request]` vs `on: push: branches: [main]` in GitHub Actions?
Write OpenTrace's PR validation workflow: on pull_request, run `golangci-lint`, `go test -race`, `govulncheck`, `tsc --noEmit`, `vitest run`.
What is a GitHub Actions matrix build? How does OpenTrace test across multiple Go versions?
Add matrix build to OpenTrace CI: `matrix: { go: ['1.22', '1.23'], os: ['ubuntu-latest'] }`. Run tests on each combination.
What is a GitHub Actions `paths` filter? How does OpenTrace skip the backend build when only UI files changed?
Add path filters to OpenTrace CI:
What is `actions/cache`? How does OpenTrace cache Go modules between runs?
Add Go module cache to OpenTrace CI:
What is GitHub Actions OIDC? How does OpenTrace authenticate to AWS without storing credentials?
Configure OIDC auth for OpenTrace CI: `aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials` with `role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::123:role/openTrace-ci`. No static keys.
What is `docker/build-push-action`? How does OpenTrace build and push Docker images in CI?
Write OpenTrace Docker build and push step:
What is branch protection in GitHub? How does OpenTrace require PR reviews and passing CI before merging?
Write GitHub branch protection rules for OpenTrace `main`: require 1 reviewer, require status checks (lint, test, trivy), no direct push.
What is a GitHub Actions environment with protection rules? How does OpenTrace require manual approval for production deploys?
Create `production` environment in GitHub Actions for OpenTrace: require review from `openTrace-ops` team, timeout after 1 hour.
What is `github.sha` vs `github.ref` in Actions? How does OpenTrace tag Docker images?
Tag OpenTrace images with both SHA and semantic version: `$ECR_URL/openTrace:${{ github.sha }}` and `$ECR_URL/openTrace:${{ github.ref_name }}` (for tags).
What is `actions/checkout` with `fetch-depth: 0`? When does OpenTrace need full git history for changelog generation?
Write OpenTrace release workflow: on git tag push, `goreleaser release --clean`, build multi-arch binaries, create GitHub release, push Docker images.
What is semantic-release? How does OpenTrace auto-generate changelogs from conventional commits?
Configure conventional commits for OpenTrace: `feat: add tail sampling`, `fix: correct span duration`, `chore: upgrade Go 1.23`. `semantic-release` generates CHANGELOG.md.
What is `reusable workflow` in GitHub Actions? How does OpenTrace create a shared Docker build workflow used by all 7 component jobs?
Write a reusable workflow for OpenTrace Docker build: inputs `component`, `dockerfile-path`, outputs `image-digest`. Called from each component's workflow.
What is `workflow_dispatch`? How does OpenTrace's ops team manually trigger a rollback workflow?
Add `workflow_dispatch` to OpenTrace's rollback workflow: input `target-sha` (the SHA to roll back to), update ECS task definition or Kubernetes image.
What is `concurrency` in GitHub Actions? How does OpenTrace cancel in-progress deploys when a new deploy is triggered?
Add concurrency to OpenTrace deploy workflow: `concurrency: { group: deploy-production, cancel-in-progress: true }`.
What is `timeout-minutes` in GitHub Actions? How does OpenTrace prevent runaway CI jobs from consuming runners indefinitely?
Add timeouts to OpenTrace CI jobs: test job `timeout-minutes: 15`, build job `timeout-minutes: 20`, deploy job `timeout-minutes: 10`.
What is a self-hosted runner? When does OpenTrace use self-hosted runners for faster builds or AWS network access?
What is `dorny/paths-filter`? How does OpenTrace detect which components changed in a monorepo?
Add path-based filtering to OpenTrace CI monorepo: only build collector when collector files change, only build UI when UI files change.
What is Turbo for monorepo CI? How does OpenTrace skip unchanged packages using Turborepo's dependency graph?
What is `gotestsum` for Go test output? How does OpenTrace format test results for GitHub Actions annotations?
Use `gotestsum` in OpenTrace CI: `gotestsum --format github-actions --junitfile results.xml -- -race -count=1 ./...`. Upload results.xml as artifact.
What is `actionlint`? How does OpenTrace lint GitHub Actions workflow files in CI?
Add actionlint to OpenTrace's CI: `actionlint .github/workflows/*.yml`. Catches type errors and missing inputs in workflow files.
What is `slsa-provenance`? How does OpenTrace generate SLSA build provenance for supply chain security?
What is `sigstore/cosign`? How does OpenTrace sign container images?
Sign OpenTrace images with cosign in CI: `cosign sign --key cosign.key $ECR_URL/openTrace:$SHA`. Verify at deploy time: `cosign verify --key cosign.pub`.
What is dependabot? How does OpenTrace automatically update Go and npm dependencies?
Configure dependabot for OpenTrace: weekly Go module updates, weekly npm updates, weekly GitHub Actions updates, auto-merge patch updates.
What is `gitleaks`? How does OpenTrace scan for accidentally committed secrets?
Add gitleaks to OpenTrace CI: `gitleaks detect --source . --verbose`. Also as a pre-commit hook: `gitleaks protect --staged`.
What is release engineering? What is a release candidate? How does OpenTrace manage versioning across 7 components?
What is `k6` cloud execution? How does OpenTrace run load tests against staging from GitHub Actions on every deploy?
Add k6 load test to OpenTrace's staging deploy pipeline: run 100 VUs for 5 minutes, assert p99 < 10ms, error rate < 0.1%. Fail deploy if thresholds not met.
What is a feature flag deployment? How does OpenTrace deploy code and enable the feature only after verifying it in production?
Integrate LaunchDarkly into OpenTrace's deploy workflow: deploy code, enable flag for 1% of traffic, monitor for 30 minutes, gradually increase to 100%.
What is zero-downtime deployment? What techniques does OpenTrace use: rolling update, blue-green, canary?
What is a rollback procedure? How fast can OpenTrace roll back: Kubernetes rollout undo (30 seconds), ECS force new deployment (2 minutes)?
Write OpenTrace's automated rollback: if deploy webhook reports error rate > 5% for 5 minutes, trigger `kubectl rollout undo`.
What is progressive delivery? What is feature flag + canary + observability-driven promotion?
What is `atlantis` for Terraform pull request automation? How does OpenTrace auto-run `terraform plan` on PR and apply on merge?
Configure atlantis for OpenTrace: auto-plan on PR, require plan comment approval, auto-apply on merge to `main`.
Design OpenTrace's complete CI/CD pipeline: PR check → merge → build → scan → push to ECR → deploy to staging → load test → manual approval → deploy to prod → monitor → auto-rollback.
What is authentication vs authorization? What is OpenTrace's auth model: JWT RS256 for users, API keys for SDKs, mTLS for services?
What is a JWT? What are the three parts? What does OpenTrace put in the payload?
What is RS256 vs HS256 for JWT? Why does OpenTrace use RS256 (asymmetric)?
Generate RS256 JWT in Go: create RSA key pair, sign with private key, verify with public key. Show the three-part token structure.
What is a refresh token? Why does it exist? How does OpenTrace implement rotation?
Implement refresh token rotation: on refresh, verify old token, issue new access token (15min) + new refresh token (7 days), revoke old refresh token in Redis.
What is parameterized SQL query? Why is `fmt.Sprintf` in SQL a critical SQL injection vulnerability?
Show SQL injection in OpenTrace:
What is HMAC webhook signature? Why does OpenTrace use it for webhook delivery verification?
Implement HMAC webhook signature in Go: `hmac.New(sha256.New, secret)`, sign body, set `X-Signature: sha256={hex}`. Verify using `hmac.Equal` (timing-safe).
What is a timing attack? Why must you use `hmac.Equal` (constant-time) instead of `==` for comparing secrets?
Demonstrate timing attack vulnerability:
What is `crypto/rand` vs `math/rand` in Go? Why must OpenTrace use `crypto/rand` for OTP generation?
Generate cryptographically secure OTP in Go: `crypto/rand.Int(rand.Reader, big.NewInt(1000000))`. Pad to 6 digits with `fmt.Sprintf("%06d", n)`.
What is API key storage? Why does OpenTrace store `SHA256(key)` not the raw key?
Implement API key creation in Go: `crypto/rand.Read(keyBytes)`, encode as `hex.EncodeToString`, store `sha256.Sum256(key)` in DB. Return raw key once.
What is `http.MaxBytesReader`? Why does OpenTrace set a 10MB request body limit?
Add body size limiting to OpenTrace: `r.Body = http.MaxBytesReader(w, r.Body, 10<<20)`. Return 413 if exceeded.
What is input validation? What is the Zod schema for OpenTrace's span submission?
Validate OpenTrace span submission with Zod (TypeScript SDK): `z.object({ trace_id: z.string().uuid(), spans: z.array(spanSchema).min(1).max(10000) })`.
What is XSS (Cross-Site Scripting)? How does React prevent it? When does `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` reintroduce the risk?
What is CSRF (Cross-Site Request Forgery)? How does `SameSite=Strict` cookie prevent it?
Set secure cookies for OpenTrace: `SameSite=Strict; Secure; HttpOnly; Max-Age=86400`. Explain what each attribute prevents.
What is CORS? How does OpenTrace configure allowed origins for the API?
Configure CORS in OpenTrace: allow `https://openTrace.dev` origin, `GET POST DELETE` methods, `Authorization Content-Type` headers. Reject all other origins.
What is CSP (Content Security Policy)? Write a CSP header for OpenTrace UI.
Write OpenTrace UI CSP: `Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' cdn.openTrace.dev; connect-src 'self' api.openTrace.dev wss://api.openTrace.dev; img-src 'self' data:`.
What is HSTS? What is HSTS preloading? How does OpenTrace enable it?
Add HSTS to OpenTrace: `Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload`. Submit domain to HSTS preload list.
What is `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff`? What is `X-Frame-Options: DENY`? What is `Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin`?
Add all security headers to OpenTrace in one middleware: `nosniff`, `X-Frame-Options: DENY`, `Referrer-Policy`, `Permissions-Policy: camera=(), microphone=()`.
What is OAuth2 PKCE? Why does OpenTrace require it for browser-based authorization flows?
Implement OAuth2 PKCE in TypeScript: `crypto.getRandomValues` for code_verifier, `crypto.subtle.digest('SHA-256', ...)` for code_challenge, send both to auth server.
What is `govulncheck`? What does it scan (Go module vulnerabilities in OSV database)? How does OpenTrace use it in CI?
Run `govulncheck ./...` on OpenTrace. Show the output format: affected symbol, vulnerability ID, severity. Add to CI with `exit 1` on any finding.
What is `trivy` image scanning? What does it find: OS package vulnerabilities, language package vulnerabilities?
Scan OpenTrace's Docker image: `trivy image --severity CRITICAL,HIGH --exit-code 1 openTrace-collector:latest`. Show typical findings in a Go + distroless image.
What is supply chain security? What is SLSA (Supply Levels for Software Artifacts)?
What is a penetration test? What is responsible disclosure? How does OpenTrace handle vulnerability reports?
What are the OWASP Top 10? Which apply to OpenTrace: injection (A03), broken auth (A07), sensitive data exposure (A02), security misconfiguration (A05)?
What is multi-factor authentication (MFA)? How does OpenTrace require TOTP for admin accounts?
Implement TOTP validation in Go: `pquerna/otp` library, verify 6-digit code against stored TOTP secret, accept ±1 time step for clock skew.
What is a secret scanning tool? What is `detect-secrets`? How does OpenTrace's pre-commit hook prevent committing secrets?
Configure pre-commit hooks for OpenTrace: `gitleaks protect --staged` (blocks if secrets detected), `golangci-lint run` (lint Go), `tsc --noEmit` (type check TS).
What is encryption at rest? How does OpenTrace encrypt ClickHouse data, PostgreSQL data, and S3 archives?
What is encryption in transit? What protocols does OpenTrace use: TLS 1.3 for HTTP, mTLS for inter-service gRPC, TLS for Kafka, SSL for PostgreSQL?
What is a WAF (Web Application Firewall)? When does OpenTrace use Cloudflare WAF in front of the Collector?
What is DDoS protection? How does Cloudflare protect OpenTrace's public Collector endpoint?
What is IAM least privilege? How does OpenTrace's ECS task role only have `s3:PutObject` on the specific bucket, not `s3:*`?
Write IAM policy for OpenTrace Collector: `s3:PutObject` on `arn:aws:s3:::openTrace-spans/*` only. `kms:GenerateDataKey` for encryption. Nothing else.
What is AWS GuardDuty? What is AWS SecurityHub? How does OpenTrace use them for threat detection?
What is an audit trail? How does OpenTrace log every API mutation for compliance: who, what, when, from where?
Implement audit logging middleware for OpenTrace: log `{timestamp, user_id, tenant_id, method, path, status, ip, trace_id}` to a separate append-only Loki stream.
What is GDPR? What is the right to erasure? How does OpenTrace implement span deletion for GDPR compliance?
Implement GDPR erasure in OpenTrace: `DELETE FROM spans WHERE user_id = $1`, invalidate all caches for that user, return 202 with job ID for async completion.
What is SOC 2? What are the five trust service criteria? What does OpenTrace need to achieve SOC 2 Type II?
What is PCI DSS? When would OpenTrace need it (if PayCore processes card data via OpenTrace's spans)? What is PCI scope reduction?
What is a security review process? What is threat modeling? How does OpenTrace do threat modeling for new features?
Write a threat model for OpenTrace's span ingestion: assets (span data), threats (injection, replay, exfiltration), controls (auth, rate limiting, TLS, HMAC).

Design OpenTrace's complete security posture: JWT auth, mTLS inter-service, parameterized SQL, HMAC webhooks, crypto/rand, HSTS, CSP, govulncheck in CI, trivy image scan, OIDC for CI/CD, Secrets Manager, IRSA, audit logs, GDPR erasure.
What are the four main AWS cost drivers for OpenTrace: compute (ECS/EKS), storage (S3/RDS/ElastiCache), network egress, managed services (MSK/RDS)?

backend.text: Calculate OpenTrace's S3 egress cost: 100TB/month of trace downloads × $0.09/GB = $9,000/month. R2 = $0 egress. Show the cost savings in ADR.

backend.text: What is data compression for cost? ClickHouse LZ4 compression achieves 8:1 ratio. How much storage does OpenTrace save vs uncompressed?

backend.text: Calculate OpenTrace ClickHouse storage cost: 10M spans/sec × 2KB = 20GB/sec uncompressed. With 8:1 LZ4 = 2.5GB/sec = 200TB/day. At $0.025/GB/month = $X/month.

backend.text: Final synthesis: You are presenting OpenTrace's infrastructure to Infraspec. In 5 minutes, cover: Docker (multi-stage, distroless, trivy), Kubernetes (7 components, HPA, PDB, Argo Rollouts), Terraform (AWS EKS + RDS + MSK + S3), CI/CD (GitHub Actions, OIDC, canary deploy), Security (JWT RS256, mTLS, govulncheck), Cost ($600/month optimized), and the SLO (99.9% availability, p99 < 10ms).

backend.text: What is Prometheus? What is its pull-based scrape model? How does it differ from push-based systems like Graphite?
What are the four Prometheus metric types: Counter, Gauge, Histogram, Summary? Give an OpenTrace example of each.
What is a Counter? Why does it only go up? What does `rate()` do to a counter in PromQL?
What is a Gauge? Give three OpenTrace examples: goroutine count, Kafka consumer lag, active WebSocket connections.
What is a Histogram? What are buckets? What is `histogram_quantile(0.99, ...)`?
What is the difference between a Histogram and a Summary? Why does Prometheus recommend Histograms for distributed systems?
Register Prometheus Counter and Histogram for OpenTrace Collector in Go using `promauto`:
Expose `/metrics` endpoint in OpenTrace: `mux.Handle("/metrics", promhttp.Handler())`.
What is label cardinality in Prometheus? Why is using `user_id` as a label dangerous?
OpenTrace's Prometheus uses 10GB RAM. The cardinality explorer shows `http_request_duration_seconds` with 50K label combinations. What is the fix?
Write PromQL for OpenTrace's 5-minute span ingestion rate: `rate(openTrace_spans_received_total[5m])`.
Write PromQL for OpenTrace's p99 span processing latency:
Write PromQL for OpenTrace's error rate: `rate(errors[5m]) / rate(total[5m])`.
What is the RED method? Write the three PromQL queries for OpenTrace Collector: Rate, Errors, Duration.
What is the USE method? Write PromQL for OpenTrace's Kafka producer: Utilization, Saturation, Errors.
Aggregate OpenTrace spans by service: `sum by (service) (rate(openTrace_spans_received_total[5m]))`.
Find top 5 services by span rate: `topk(5, sum by (service) (rate(openTrace_spans_received_total[5m])))`.
What is `rate()` vs `irate()` vs `increase()` in PromQL? When does OpenTrace use each?
Alert when OpenTrace Collector stops reporting: `absent(up{job="openTrace-collector"})`.
Predict OpenTrace ClickHouse disk full: `predict_linear(node_filesystem_free_bytes[24h], 7*24*3600) < 0`.
What is a recording rule in Prometheus? How does OpenTrace use them to pre-compute expensive per-service error rates?
Write a recording rule for OpenTrace: `record: job:openTrace_error_rate:ratio5m expr: rate(errors[5m]) / rate(total[5m])`.
What is a Prometheus scrape config? What is `scrape_interval`? Write the Kubernetes service discovery config for OpenTrace.
What is remote write? How does OpenTrace send metrics to Grafana Cloud (Mimir)?
Configure Prometheus remote write for OpenTrace: `url: https://prometheus-prod.grafana.net/api/prom/push` with basic auth.
What is Thanos? What is Mimir? How does OpenTrace use either for long-term metrics retention?
Write ServiceMonitor for OpenTrace Collector (Prometheus Operator): selector by `app: openTrace-collector`, port `metrics`, interval `15s`.
Write PrometheusRule CRD for OpenTrace's span ingestion down alert: `expr: rate(openTrace_spans_received_total[5m]) < 100 for: 5m`.
What is Alertmanager? What is a route? What is inhibition? How does OpenTrace route P1 alerts to PagerDuty?
Write Alertmanager routing: `severity=critical` → PagerDuty, `severity=warning` → Slack. Inhibit warning when critical fires.
What is an SLO? What is an error budget? How does OpenTrace calculate its monthly error budget from a 99.9% SLO?
Write multi-window burn rate alert for OpenTrace (99.9% SLO): fire when error rate × time > 5% of monthly budget in 1h AND 5m.
Write PromQL for pod restart rate in OpenTrace: `increase(kube_pod_container_status_restarts_total{namespace="openTrace"}[1h]) > 3`.
Write PromQL for host disk usage alert: `(node_filesystem_size_bytes - node_filesystem_free_bytes) / node_filesystem_size_bytes > 0.8`.
What is an exemplar in Prometheus? How does OpenTrace link a high-latency metric sample to a specific `trace_id`?
Add exemplar to OpenTrace's latency observation: `histogram.ObserveWithExemplar(duration, prometheus.Labels{"trace_id": traceID})`.
What is a native histogram in Prometheus 2.40+? How does it improve on classic histograms for dynamic bucket selection?
What is `metric_relabel_configs`? How does OpenTrace drop high-cardinality `user_id` labels before Prometheus ingestion?
What is `kube-state-metrics`? What metrics does it expose for OpenTrace: pod restarts, deployment replicas, PVC capacity?
Export Go runtime metrics from OpenTrace automatically: `import _ "github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus/collectors"` — registers GC, goroutine, memory metrics.
What is `GaugeFunc` in Prometheus Go client? Write one for OpenTrace's `runtime.NumGoroutine()`.
What is label-replace in PromQL? How does OpenTrace normalize service name labels that differ across clusters?
Compare current vs week-ago span rate in OpenTrace: `rate(openTrace_spans_received_total[5m]) / rate(openTrace_spans_received_total[5m] offset 1w)`.
What is `subquery` in PromQL? How does OpenTrace calculate the max error rate over the last 24 hours?
Calculate 24-hour max error rate for OpenTrace: `max_over_time(rate(openTrace_errors_total[5m])[24h:5m])`.
What is the difference between `sum(rate())` and `rate(sum())`? Which is correct for multi-instance error rate?
What is Prometheus Agent Mode? When does OpenTrace use it to send metrics without local storage?
What is VictoriaMetrics? How does it compare to Prometheus for OpenTrace's cardinality requirements at 10M spans/sec scale?
Secure OpenTrace's `/metrics` endpoint: serve on port 9090 bound to `127.0.0.1` only. Use Kubernetes Service to expose to Prometheus within the cluster.
Design OpenTrace's complete metrics strategy: 15 essential metrics, their types, labels, PromQL queries, recording rules, and alert thresholds.
Write PromQL for Kafka consumer lag per partition: `kafka_consumer_group_lag{group="openTrace-processor", topic="spans"}`.
Write PromQL for OpenTrace's goroutine count alert: `go_goroutines{job="openTrace-collector"} > 50000 for: 5m`.
Write PromQL for ClickHouse insert batch size p99: `histogram_quantile(0.99, rate(openTrace_clickhouse_batch_size_bucket[5m]))`.
Write PromQL for OpenTrace Redis hit rate: `rate(redis_hits_total[5m]) / (rate(redis_hits_total[5m]) + rate(redis_misses_total[5m]))`.
Alert when OpenTrace's Redis eviction rate is high: `rate(redis_evicted_keys_total[5m]) > 100`.

Write Helm values for OpenTrace's Prometheus: retention 15d, storage 50Gi, scrape interval 15s, remote write to Grafana Cloud.

Alert when OpenTrace metrics scrape takes too long: `scrape_duration_seconds{job="openTrace-collector"} > 10`.
