backend.text: Design the go-to-market strategy for OpenTrace's AI features: (1) free tier (10 AI explanations/day), (2) Pro ($99/month, 500/day), (3) Enterprise (custom pricing, self-hosted LLM option, GDPR compliance package). What feature gates exist at each tier?
backend.subSection: PART E — Production AI & Full Synthesis (Q401–Q500)

backend.text: Walk through the interview story for OpenTrace's AI features: "I noticed that engineers using OpenTrace spent 80% of incident time on data gathering (looking at metrics, logs, traces) and only 20% on actual reasoning. So I built an AI layer that automates the data gathering using an agent with 4 tools, and uses RAG to surface similar historical incidents. This cut mean diagnosis time from 45 minutes to 8 minutes."
backend.subSection: PART E — Production AI & Full Synthesis (Q401–Q500)

backend.text: What is `context engineering`? Why is it considered more important than prompt engineering for production AI systems like OpenTrace?
backend.subSection: PART E — Production AI & Full Synthesis (Q401–Q500)

backend.text: Design OpenTrace's context engineering strategy: for each AI diagnosis, gather exactly the right context — current trace (always), similar historical incidents (Qdrant, top 3), recent Prometheus metrics for affected service (last 1h), relevant Loki errors (last 30 min). Cap at 3000 tokens total.
backend.subSection: PART E — Production AI & Full Synthesis (Q401–Q500)

backend.text: What is `evaluation-driven development` for AI? How does OpenTrace build AI features by writing evals first, then iterating until evals pass — similar to TDD?
backend.subSection: PART E — Production AI & Full Synthesis (Q401–Q500)

backend.text: Write an OpenTrace AI eval using `promptfoo`: 20 test cases covering different anomaly types (DB issues, network issues, memory issues, external dependencies), assert that each diagnosis contains the correct root cause category.
backend.subSection: PART E — Production AI & Full Synthesis (Q401–Q500)

backend.text: What is `Constitutional AI`? What is `RLAIF`? How could OpenTrace use AI feedback (LLM-as-judge) to create a self-improving diagnosis system without manual labeling?
backend.subSection: PART E — Production AI & Full Synthesis (Q401–Q500)

backend.text: Design OpenTrace's long-term AI product vision: year 1 (reactive — explain what happened), year 2 (proactive — predict before it happens), year 3 (autonomous — self-heal common issues without human intervention), year 4 (strategic — recommend architectural improvements based on incident patterns).
backend.subSection: PART E — Production AI & Full Synthesis (Q401–Q500)

backend.text: Describe how OpenTrace would demonstrate AI value to a skeptical CTO: "Run a 30-day blind trial — 50% of incidents get AI diagnosis, 50% don't. Measure MTTR for each group. We expect 35-minute improvement. Cost: $310/month. Conservatively worth $50K/month in engineering time saved."
backend.subSection: PART E — Production AI & Full Synthesis (Q401–Q500)

backend.text: Walk through the technical challenges of building OpenTrace's AI features that you would mention in an Infraspec interview: (1) multi-tenant vector isolation (Qdrant filter design), (2) prompt injection from span data, (3) hallucination in diagnosis (mitigated by grounding), (4) latency budget with streaming LLM, (5) cost at scale (token optimization + caching).
backend.subSection: PART E — Production AI & Full Synthesis (Q401–Q500)

backend.text: Design the OpenTrace AI contribution to open source: publish `go-observability-ai` library on GitHub — a Go SDK that adds AI diagnosis to any OpenTelemetry-compatible observability backend. Include: Qdrant client, OpenAI integration, RAG pipeline, structured output parsing. Apache 2.0 license.
backend.subSection: PART E — Production AI & Full Synthesis (Q401–Q500)

backend.text: Final synthesis: You are at your Infraspec technical interview. Walk through the complete OpenTrace system in 10 minutes: architecture (7 components), observability (Prometheus + Loki + Tempo + Grafana), testing (unit → integration → E2E → load → chaos), and GenAI features (RAG diagnosis with Qdrant + GPT-4o, agent with 4 tools, semantic search, cost $310/month). End with: "The AI layer is what transforms OpenTrace from another Jaeger clone into a product that actually reduces MTTR by 80% — and that's the story I'm most proud of."
backend.subSection: PART E — Production AI & Full Synthesis (Q401–Q500)

clrs.text: What does O(n) notation mean precisely? Give the formal definition involving constants c and n₀.
What is the difference between O, Ω, Θ, o, and ω? When does Θ(n) not exist for an algorithm?
Why is O-notation an upper bound but not always tight? Give an example where O(n²) is technically correct but Θ(n log n) is better.
Prove that n² + 100n + 5 = Θ(n²). Find explicit constants c₁, c₂, and n₀.
What is the difference between worst-case, average-case, and best-case complexity? Which does O-notation describe?
Rank the following from slowest to fastest growth: n!, 2ⁿ, nⁿ, n³, n² log n, n log n, n, log n, log log n, 1.
What is amortized complexity? How is it different from average-case complexity?
Prove that log(n!) = Θ(n log n) using Stirling's approximation.
What does "polynomial time" mean? Why is it the boundary between "tractable" and "intractable" in complexity theory?
Is 2^(2n) = O(2^n)? Prove or disprove.
What is space complexity? Can an O(n log n) time algorithm use O(1) space?
What is a loop invariant? How do you use it to prove algorithm correctness?
Prove the loop invariant for insertion sort: at the start of each iteration, the subarray A[1..j-1] contains the original elements in sorted order.
What is the difference between an algorithm's time complexity and its practical running time?
Show that if f(n) = O(g(n)) and g(n) = O(h(n)), then f(n) = O(h(n)). (Transitivity of O.)
What is the RAM model of computation? What operations cost O(1) and what are its limitations?
Prove that max(f(n), g(n)) = Θ(f(n) + g(n)).
Why does constant factor matter in practice even if it doesn't affect asymptotic complexity?
What is the difference between O(n) and O(n) expected? Why does expected complexity need careful framing?
Show that n^k = O(c^n) for any k > 0 and c > 1. (Polynomials are eventually dominated by exponentials.)
What is a tight bound? What does it mean to say an algorithm is "optimally efficient"?
Prove the lower bound Ω(n log n) for comparison-based sorting using decision trees.
What is recursion depth and how does it affect space complexity?
Prove that log_a(n) = Θ(log_b(n)) for any constants a, b > 1 — i.e., the base of logarithm doesn't matter asymptotically.
What is the practical impact of cache behavior on algorithm performance when two algorithms have the same asymptotic complexity?
An algorithm runs in O(n log n). Your input doubles from 10⁶ to 2×10⁶. By how much does runtime increase (approximately)?
You have two sorting algorithms: A runs in 3n log n operations, B runs in 0.5n² operations. For what input sizes is A faster?
When is an O(n²) algorithm acceptable in production? Give three practical scenarios.
What is iterated logarithm (log*n)? What algorithm has O(n log* n) complexity?
What is the definition of o(g(n)) ("little-o")? Prove that n = o(n log n).
What is a recurrence relation? Give the recurrence for merge sort and state its solution.
Solve T(n) = 2T(n/2) + n using the substitution method. Prove T(n) = O(n log n).
Solve T(n) = T(n-1) + n using substitution. What is the closed form?
State the Master Theorem. What are the three cases and their conditions?
Apply the Master Theorem to: T(n) = 4T(n/2) + n. Which case applies? What is the solution?
Apply the Master Theorem to: T(n) = 4T(n/2) + n². Which case applies?
Apply the Master Theorem to: T(n) = 4T(n/2) + n³. Which case applies?
Apply the Master Theorem to: T(n) = 2T(n/2) + n log n. Does the Master Theorem apply? Why not?
Use the recursion tree method to solve T(n) = 3T(n/4) + Θ(n²).
Use the recursion tree method to solve T(n) = T(n/3) + T(2n/3) + O(n). (Quicksort worst/balanced split.)
What is the Akra-Bazzi theorem? When do you use it instead of the Master Theorem?
Solve the recurrence T(n) = T(n-1) + T(n-2) + 1, which resembles Fibonacci. What is the asymptotic growth?
Prove by induction that the solution to T(n) = 2T(n/2) + n is O(n log n). Show the inductive step clearly.
Solve T(n) = T(√n) + 1 using the change of variables m = log n.
What is the recurrence for binary search? Solve it.
What is the recurrence for Strassen's matrix multiplication? Solve it using the Master Theorem.
Solve T(n) = 2T(n/2) + Θ(1). What algorithm has this recurrence?
What is a "smoothness condition" in recurrence solving? Why does it matter?
Solve T(n) = T(n-k) + O(n) for constant k. What algorithm pattern gives this?
Prove that the solution to T(n) = aT(n/b) + f(n) in Case 1 of the Master Theorem is Θ(n^(log_b a)).
What is the recurrence for the Karatsuba multiplication algorithm? Solve it. How does it compare to the naïve O(n²)?
Solve T(n) = 4T(n/2) + n² log n. (Hint: Master Theorem doesn't directly apply — use recursion tree.)
What is memoization? How does it convert an exponential recursive algorithm into polynomial time?
Fibonacci naïve recursion has recurrence T(n) = T(n-1) + T(n-2) + O(1). What is its complexity? How does memoization reduce it?
For a recurrence T(n) = T(n/3) + T(2n/3) + n, draw the recursion tree and determine the solution.
What is the Characteristic equation method for solving linear recurrences? Give an example.
What is the recurrence for the closest pair of points algorithm? Solve it.
Solve T(n) = 2T(n/4) + √n using the Master Theorem. Which case applies?
Why does merge sort have O(n log n) runtime but O(n) space, while heapsort has O(n log n) time and O(1) space?
Prove the lower bound for recurrence solving: any algorithm that uses substitution must still "guess" the form. What technique avoids guessing?
What are the three steps of a divide-and-conquer algorithm? Give a concrete example for each step using merge sort.
Write the merge sort algorithm. What is the time and space complexity?
Write the binary search algorithm iteratively and recursively. Prove correctness using a loop invariant.
Write the algorithm for finding the maximum subarray using divide and conquer. What is its recurrence?
What is Strassen's algorithm? What does it improve over the naïve O(n³) matrix multiplication?
Derive Strassen's recurrence T(n) = 7T(n/2) + Θ(n²). What is the solution?
Implement the closest pair of points algorithm in O(n log n). What is the key insight of the strip step?
How would you use divide and conquer to count inversions in an array in O(n log n)?
How would you use divide and conquer to find the kth largest element? What is the worst-case complexity?
Merge sort vs. quicksort in practice — both O(n log n) average. Which do you use and why?
Write the algorithm for multiplying two n-bit integers using Karatsuba's method. What is its complexity?
How do you compute a^n mod m efficiently using divide and conquer? What is the time complexity?
What is the "combine" step in merge sort? Why is it O(n) and not O(1)?
Given a sorted rotated array, how do you binary search for a target? What modification to standard binary search is needed?
Implement a divide and conquer algorithm to compute the power set of a set of n elements.
How would you solve the "majority element" problem (element appearing > n/2 times) using divide and conquer in O(n)?
What is the relationship between divide and conquer and dynamic programming? When does D&C become DP (overlapping subproblems)?
For matrix multiplication, when would you use Strassen in practice? What are the constant factor and numerical stability tradeoffs?
How do you find the second smallest element in an array using divide and conquer with at most n + log n - 2 comparisons?
Prove that the maximum subarray problem cannot be solved faster than O(n) — i.e., Ω(n) is a lower bound.
What is a randomized algorithm? What is the difference between Las Vegas and Monte Carlo algorithms?
What is expected running time? Is it the same as average-case running time?
What is the expected number of comparisons in randomized quicksort? Derive it using indicator random variables.
What is indicator random variable? How does CLRS use it to simplify expected value calculations?
Prove that the expected number of heads in n fair coin flips is n/2 using linearity of expectation.
What is the birthday paradox? If there are n people, what is the probability that two share a birthday? At what n does this exceed 50%?
How would you use the birthday paradox to design a hash function analysis?
What is the hiring problem (secretary problem)? What is the optimal strategy and what is its expected cost?
In the hiring problem, derive that the expected number of hires is Θ(ln n).
What is a random permutation? How does the RANDOMIZE-IN-PLACE algorithm work and what is its correctness argument?
Prove that RANDOMIZE-IN-PLACE produces a uniformly random permutation.
What is the difference between randomized quicksort and quicksort with median-of-3 pivot? Which is better in practice?
How does randomization help with adversarial inputs to quicksort?
What is the expected number of recursive calls in randomized quicksort? Use indicator variables.
What is a probabilistic recurrence? Give an example from randomized selection.
How would you sample k items uniformly at random from a stream of n items without knowing n in advance? (Reservoir sampling)
Implement reservoir sampling for k=1. Prove it samples each element with probability 1/n.
What is Markov's inequality? What is Chebyshev's inequality? How are they used to bound tail probabilities of randomized algorithms?
When is a randomized algorithm preferable to a deterministic one with the same expected complexity?
A hash table uses a random hash function. Use the birthday paradox to determine the expected number of elements before the first collision.
What is insertion sort? What is its time complexity in best, average, and worst case? When would you choose it over merge sort?
Write insertion sort. Prove its loop invariant.
What is selection sort? What is its complexity? Why is it rarely used in practice?
What is bubble sort? What is its complexity? Why does it perform poorly despite being simple?
Write merge sort. Trace through sorting [5, 2, 8, 1, 9, 3].
Prove merge sort's correctness by induction on the array length.
What is heapsort? What is its time and space complexity? How does it compare to merge sort?
Write the MAX-HEAPIFY procedure. What is its time complexity and why?
Write the BUILD-MAX-HEAP procedure. Why is its complexity O(n) and not O(n log n)?
Prove that BUILD-MAX-HEAP runs in O(n) time using the sum of heights in a complete binary tree.
Write the full HEAPSORT algorithm combining BUILD-MAX-HEAP and EXTRACT-MAX.
What is a max-heap property? How does heapsort use it?
Heapsort vs. merge sort: both O(n log n). What are the cache, stability, and space tradeoffs?
What does it mean for a sorting algorithm to be stable? Name one stable and one unstable sorting algorithm.
Write randomized quicksort (with random pivot selection). What is its expected complexity?
What is the worst case for quicksort? Give an input that triggers it. What is its recurrence?
Prove that the expected running time of randomized quicksort is O(n log n) using indicator random variables.
What is the PARTITION procedure in quicksort? Write the Lomuto and Hoare partition schemes.
Compare Lomuto vs. Hoare partition. Which does fewer swaps?
What is the three-way partition (Dutch National Flag problem)? When does it make quicksort O(n) for special inputs?
Implement introsort (introspective sort). Why does Python use Timsort instead?
What is Timsort? How does it exploit natural runs in real-world data?
Prove the Ω(n log n) lower bound for comparison-based sorting using a decision tree argument.
What is the decision tree model? How does it capture comparison-based sorting?
The decision tree for sorting n elements has at least n! leaves. What height must it have? Use Stirling's approximation to show Ω(n log n).
In what scenarios is quicksort faster than merge sort in practice, despite identical asymptotic complexity?
When sorting nearly sorted data, which algorithm is fastest: insertion sort, merge sort, or quicksort?
What is shellsort? What is its best known time complexity?
Implement merge sort iteratively (bottom-up). Compare it to the recursive version.
When would you use an in-place sorting algorithm vs. one with auxiliary space?
Sort an array of 1 million integers where each integer is between 0 and 1000. What is the most efficient approach?
Given an array of n elements, k of which are unsorted (the rest are in sorted order), find the optimal sorting algorithm and its complexity.
Implement sort for a linked list. Can you use in-place quicksort? Why or why not?
Given two sorted arrays of size m and n, merge them in O(m + n) time and O(1) space.
What is the information-theoretic lower bound? Why can't any comparison-based sort beat O(n log n)?
Implement an O(n log n) algorithm to find the number of inversions in an array using merge sort.
You need to sort 1TB of data that doesn't fit in RAM. What algorithm do you use?
What is external sorting? What is the merge-sort-based approach for disk-based data?
Implement k-way merge (merging k sorted arrays) using a min-heap.
What is the fastest way to find the top-k elements from n unsorted elements?
What is an adaptive sorting algorithm? What property of the input does it exploit?
Implement cycle sort — the algorithm that minimizes the number of writes to the array.
Prove that any sorting algorithm that uses only comparisons must make at least ⌈log₂(n!)⌉ comparisons in the worst case.
How do you sort an array that has only two distinct values (0s and 1s) in O(n) time and O(1) space?
Implement the Dutch National Flag algorithm to sort an array with three distinct values (0, 1, 2).
How would you sort a sequence of strings lexicographically most efficiently?
What is the AKS sorting network? What is its significance?
Given a stream of integers, maintain the top-k elements at all times. What data structure and what is the complexity per element?
Implement patience sorting. What well-known algorithm does it relate to?
In Python, `list.sort()` uses Timsort. In C++, `std::sort` uses introsort. When would you need to implement your own sort?
What is counting sort? What is its time complexity? What assumption does it make?
Write counting sort. Trace through sorting [3, 1, 4, 1, 5, 9, 2, 6] with k=9.
Prove that counting sort is stable. Why does stability matter here?
What is radix sort? How does it use counting sort as a subroutine?
Write radix sort for integers. What is its time complexity in terms of n and the number of digits d?
When is radix sort asymptotically faster than comparison-based sorts? What does d need to be?
What is bucket sort? What distribution assumption does it require?
Implement bucket sort for floating point numbers uniform in [0,1). Prove its expected O(n) running time.
Derive the expected running time of bucket sort using indicator random variables and the birthday paradox.
When would you use bucket sort, counting sort, or radix sort instead of comparison-based sort?
Prove that the Ω(n log n) lower bound does NOT apply to counting sort. What assumption does this violate?
Sort 10 million IP addresses (32-bit integers). What is the most efficient approach?
Sort 1 million dates (in format YYYYMMDD) efficiently. What algorithm and what is the complexity?
Given an array of n integers each in range [1, n²], sort them in O(n) time.
Implement LSD (Least Significant Digit) radix sort vs. MSD radix sort. When does MSD enable early termination?
How would you sort a list of strings using radix sort? What alphabet size matters?
What is the ALGORITHM X bound by van Emde Boas for sorting integers in O(n log log n)?
Radix sort on integers vs. comparison sort: at what n does radix sort win on modern hardware?
Sort an array of n integers in range [1, n^k] for constant k. What is the time complexity of radix sort here?
Implement stable counting sort that can be used as a subroutine in radix sort. Why must it be stable?
What is the selection problem? What is the difference between finding the minimum and the kth smallest element?
Write the RANDOMIZED-SELECT algorithm. What is its expected time complexity?
Prove that RANDOMIZED-SELECT runs in O(n) expected time.
Write the deterministic SELECT algorithm (median of medians). What is its worst-case time complexity?
Derive the recurrence for the median-of-medians algorithm: T(n) ≤ T(n/5) + T(7n/10 + 6) + O(n). Solve it.
Prove that at least 3n/10 - 6 elements are less than the median of medians, ensuring a good split.
RANDOMIZED-SELECT vs. median of medians: both O(n), but which is used in practice and why?
Find the median of two sorted arrays in O(log(m+n)) time. What is the key invariant?
Implement the median of two sorted arrays algorithm. Trace through an example.
Find the kth smallest element in an unsorted array in O(n) worst case. Which algorithm?
Implement a data structure that supports: insert(x), find_median(). What is the complexity of each operation?
Sliding window median: given a stream of numbers and a window of size k, find the median at each step.
What is the nth-element algorithm in C++ STL? What guarantee does it provide?
What is the "tournament tree" approach to finding the minimum? How many comparisons does it take?
Prove that finding the minimum of n elements requires exactly n-1 comparisons. Prove the lower bound.
Finding both minimum and maximum: the naïve approach uses 2n-2 comparisons. What is the optimal algorithm? How many comparisons does it use?
Write the simultaneous min-max algorithm that uses at most 3⌊n/2⌋ comparisons.
Given an unsorted array of n numbers, find the two closest numbers in O(n log n) time.
Given a set of n points, find the point closest to the median in O(n) time.
What is the "AKS" decision tree lower bound for the selection problem?
How do you find the kth smallest element in a sorted matrix (n×n) efficiently?
Given n sorted lists each of length k, find the overall median in O(n log(nk)) time.
Find the top-k largest elements in an unsorted array. What is the most efficient algorithm?
Implement a weighted median algorithm where each element has a weight.
The "1-center" problem: given n points on a line, find the point that minimizes the maximum distance to any of them. What is the answer?
Find the median in a stream of numbers in O(log n) per insertion using two heaps.
Quickselect (RANDOMIZED-SELECT) vs. a sorting-based approach for finding the median. Compare constants.
Prove the lower bound for the selection problem: finding the kth smallest element requires Ω(n) comparisons.
How would you use order statistics to implement a data structure that supports rank(x) and select(k) queries?
What is the "finger search tree"? How does it improve on binary search for near-predecessor queries?
What is the difference between an array and a linked list for implementing a stack? What are the tradeoffs?
Implement a queue using two stacks. What is the amortized time per operation?
Implement a stack that supports push, pop, and getMin in O(1) time per operation.
Implement a doubly linked list with insert, delete, and search. What is the time complexity of each?
Detect a cycle in a linked list in O(n) time and O(1) space. (Floyd's tortoise and hare.)
Reverse a linked list iteratively and recursively.
What is a sentinel node in a linked list? How does it simplify boundary conditions?
Implement an LRU cache using a doubly linked list + hash map. What are the time complexities?
Merge k sorted linked lists in O(n log k) time. What data structure do you use?
Find the middle of a linked list in one pass.
Implement a deque (double-ended queue) with O(1) push and pop from both ends.
Given a string, use a stack to check if parentheses are balanced.
Implement a circular buffer (ring buffer). What happens on overflow?
Use a monotonic stack to find the next greater element for each element in an array in O(n).
Implement a skip list. What is the expected time complexity for search, insert, delete?
What is a hash table? What is the expected O(1) time complexity predicated on?
What is a collision in a hash table? Name two collision resolution strategies.
Implement separate chaining for collision resolution. What is the worst-case time for search?
Implement open addressing with linear probing. What is the load factor α and why does it matter?
What is clustering in open addressing? What is primary clustering vs. secondary clustering?
What is quadratic probing? How does it reduce primary clustering?
What is double hashing? Why is it the preferred open addressing strategy?
Prove that for a hash table with load factor α < 1 using open addressing, the expected number of probes for a successful search is at most (1/α) ln(1/(1-α)).
What is a good hash function? What properties should it have?
What is universal hashing? What does it guarantee against adversarial inputs?
Prove that a universal hash family guarantees O(1) expected time per operation regardless of input.
What is perfect hashing? When is it useful and what is the two-level construction?
What is the load factor? How does it affect hash table performance? When do you resize?
Implement a hash table with dynamic resizing (doubling when α > 0.75). What is the amortized cost of insert?
What is consistent hashing? How is it used in distributed systems?
Design a hash function for strings. What is the polynomial rolling hash?
Two strings are anagrams of each other. How do you check in O(n) using hashing?
Find the first non-repeating character in a string using a hash table.
Find all pairs of integers in an array that sum to a target value. Use a hash table for O(n).
Group anagrams together from a list of strings. What is the hashing strategy?
Implement a LRU cache using `OrderedDict` (linked hash map). What is each operation's complexity?
Detect duplicates in an array in O(n) time and O(n) space using hashing.
What is cuckoo hashing? What is its worst-case lookup time?
What is a Bloom filter? How does it use multiple hash functions? What can it tell you with certainty?
Derive the optimal number of hash functions for a Bloom filter with n elements and m bits to minimize false positive rate.
How would you implement a spell checker using a Bloom filter?
Hash table vs. balanced BST for a dictionary: what are the performance tradeoffs?
What is the FKS scheme (Fredman-Komlós-Szemerédi)? How does it achieve O(1) worst-case lookups?
Use polynomial hashing (Rabin-Karp) to check if two strings are equal in O(1) after O(n) preprocessing.
Implement a hash map that handles arbitrary key types. What interface must the key type provide?
What is a binary search tree? What is the BST property?
Write TREE-SEARCH, TREE-INSERT, and TREE-DELETE for a BST.
What is the expected height of a randomly built BST with n keys? Prove it is O(log n).
What is the worst-case height of a BST? What input causes it?
Write the in-order, pre-order, and post-order traversal of a BST. What does in-order produce?
Find the successor and predecessor of a node in a BST. What is the time complexity?
Find the lowest common ancestor (LCA) of two nodes in a BST.
Convert a BST to a sorted doubly linked list in-place (Morris traversal).
Validate whether a binary tree is a valid BST. What is the trap with a naïve recursive check?
Find the kth smallest element in a BST in O(log n) time with augmentation, or O(h) without.
Serialize and deserialize a BST. What is the minimum information needed?
Given a sorted array, build a height-balanced BST in O(n).
Find all elements in a BST in range [lo, hi] efficiently.
What is a treap? How does it combine BST and heap properties? What is its expected height?
Implement a treap with insert, delete, and search. Use random priorities.
How many distinct BSTs can be formed with n keys? (Catalan numbers)
Derive the recurrence for the number of BSTs with n keys. Show it equals the nth Catalan number C(n).
What is an optimal BST? How does DP solve it in O(n³)?
Implement the DP solution for optimal BST from CLRS Section 14.5.
BST vs. hash table vs. sorted array for a dictionary with range queries. When does each win?
Given two BSTs, merge them into a single sorted list in O(m+n).
Find the distance between two nodes in a BST (number of edges in the path).
What is a splay tree? What property does splaying maintain? What is its amortized complexity?
Describe the zig, zig-zig, and zig-zag cases in a splay tree rotation.
Implement `floor(x)` and `ceil(x)` for a BST — finding the greatest key ≤ x and least key ≥ x.
What are the five properties of a red-black tree? What do they guarantee about tree height?
Prove that a red-black tree with n nodes has height at most 2 lg(n+1).
What is the black-height of a node? Why is it a useful concept?
Write the LEFT-ROTATE and RIGHT-ROTATE operations. How do they preserve the BST property?
Write the RB-INSERT procedure including the fixup cases. What are the three cases in RB-INSERT-FIXUP?
Write the RB-DELETE procedure and its fixup. What are the cases in RB-DELETE-FIXUP?
Prove that RB-INSERT performs at most O(log n) rotations.
Prove that RB-DELETE performs at most a constant number of color changes (amortized).
Red-black tree vs. AVL tree: which is faster for lookups? Which is faster for inserts? Why?
What is an AVL tree? What invariant does it maintain? How does its height compare to a red-black tree?
Java's `TreeMap` is implemented with a red-black tree. What O(log n) operations does it support?
